ac_uid_algorithm: sha256(type + given + when + then + json.dumps(check, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False))[:16]

items.when: Each file is read AND pytest tests/generated/20260520-221452/ -q is run
items.then: Zero files contain bare 'pass' as sole body; zero contain 'TEST_INCOMPLETE:' sentinel; zero contain vacuous assertions (assert True, assert 1 == 1, assert constant); zero use pytest.skip or @pytest.mark.skip; each test body references the target file/path/symbol named in its docstring GIVEN clause; each test body contains at least one assertion exercising the THEN clause specific predicate (not just file existence)
Detection logic states CJK detection (U+4E00-U+9FFF) runs unconditionally in scoped files; exemption clauses suppress reporting only, not detection

items.given: agents/ba.md Quality Standards checklist is read
items.then: A checklist item exists verifying complexity_tier and risk_level fields are populated AND that the test-writer trigger gate (complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high) is correctly evaluated

items.given: scripts/blast-radius-tool.py is read AND blast-radius-tool.py --git-diff --output <tmp> is invoked on a representative working tree
items.when: The git_diff_files function is inspected AND the output JSON is parsed
items.then: Function unions git diff --name-only with git ls-files --others --exclude-standard, deduplicates, in_scope filtering; output JSON preserves all required top-level keys; edges[] deduplicated by (from, to) tuple; edges[] capped at documented per-file limit with overflow in omitted_edges_count; output JSON for typical Phase 2 run (5-10 modified files) under 500KB
The trigger explicitly checks complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high, mirroring the dispatch gate in commands/dev.md Step 8

items.given: scripts/canary-verify.sh verify_write_guard and verify_git_privilege_guard functions are read; canary-verify.sh is executed in a sandboxed environment
items.when: The negative-payload test logic is inspected AND the canary is run
items.then: verify_write_guard contains a deny-payload test (Write payload targeting an EXISTING file created via mktemp, since the guard blocks overwriting existing files per hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:120-128) and asserts rc==2; verify_git_privilege_guard contains a deny-payload test (destructive git command e.g. 'git push --force') and asserts rc==2; both functions emit_failure when their respective deny-payload is NOT blocked

check.runtime_check: canary-verify.sh exit code is 0 when all hooks correctly enforce denies

items.given: hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py and hooks/doc_sync/tree.py are read AND a fixture tree containing cp-state-foo.json + spec-20260524-test.json is provided
items.when: The skip logic is inspected AND doc_sync is run against the fixture
items.then: Both files contain executable suppression logic (not comments) for BOTH cp-state- prefix AND spec-2026 prefix patterns; generated README and INDEX tree output exclude any file matching either pattern; suppression uses startswith or equivalent prefix-aware filtering (not exact-name set membership)

check.fixture_check: fixture files matching cp-state-* and spec-2026* are absent from generated tree/README output

items.then: The list contains a prefix matching blast-radius-map.json writes (e.g., '*/.claude/dev-registry/*/blast-radius-map' or equivalent glob)

items.when: All echo and printf calls are inspected via a two-stage check
items.then: Stage 1 collects all lines matching 'echo|printf'; stage 2 filters to those NOT containing '>&2' or '1>&2'; the stage-2 filtered list MUST be empty (every echo/printf has an explicit stderr redirect); trailing comments after the redirect are allowed; the simple regex pattern ending in [^>]$ is FORBIDDEN as the gate because it false-positives on lines ending in '2'

items.given: commands/dev.md lines 284-303 are read (the 'Location-complaint tokenization procedure' block ONLY)
items.when: The location-complaint overlap procedure is inspected; the Contract D novelty extension block at lines 304-310 is also read for preservation verification
items.then: Within lines 284-303 the mechanical tokenize + set-intersection + Jaccard + domain-token table is replaced with an LLM-judgment instruction directing the orchestrator to assess semantic overlap using natural language reasoning; the adjacent Contract D novelty extension block at lines 304-310 (which independently uses Jaccard for layer/target_location novelty) is PRESERVED VERBATIM

check.scope_preserved: lines 304-310 (Contract D novelty extension) MUST contain Jaccard verbatim — its preservation is verified separately
